metadata.title: Advertising coordination + content-moderation-via-infrastructure (collective action as censorship/liability lever)
metadata.provenance: Court filings, House Judiciary report, company statements, Reuters/FT/AP-tier.

mechanisms.name: GARM brand-safety standards
mechanisms.actor: World Federation of Advertisers / GARM + advertisers (Unilever, Mars, CVS)
mechanisms.target: Platforms incl. X, podcasts, news outlets
mechanisms.outcome: Common harmful-content taxonomy; House Judiciary Jul 2024 report alleged collusion; GARM discontinued Aug 8 2024, days after X's suit
mechanisms.coordination_grade: Documented coordinated standard-setting; NO adjudicated collusion

mechanisms.name: X / Rumble antitrust suit vs WFA/GARM
mechanisms.target: WFA + advertisers (Unilever, Mars, CVS, Nestle, Lego, Shell, Tyson, Pinterest)
mechanisms.outcome: Filed Aug 6 2024; expanded Feb 2025; DISMISSED WITH PREJUDICE Mar 26 2026 (Judge Jane Boyle); appeal denied
mechanisms.coordination_grade: Court: lawful parallel/independent conduct, not antitrust collusion

mechanisms.name: Collective Shout payment-processor campaign
mechanisms.outcome: Steam onboarding rule ~Jul 16 2025 (hundreds of adult games removed); itch.io deindexed ~20,000+ NSFW titles Jul 24; re-indexed FREE NSFW Aug 1; Mastercard denied ordering removals
mechanisms.coordination_grade: NGO->processor->platform chain; deniable (each layer disclaims authorship) - the cleanest payment-chokepoint-as-censor case

mechanisms.name: Stop Hate for Profit Facebook boycott
mechanisms.outcome: July 2020 ad pause; FB Holocaust-denial ban Oct 2020; negligible revenue impact
mechanisms.coordination_grade: Openly coordinated public advocacy; lawful by design

mechanisms.name: GEC funding of GDI/NewsGuard (censorship-by-proxy)
mechanisms.actor: US State Dept Global Engagement Center -> GDI, NewsGuard
mechanisms.target: Conservative outlets (Daily Wire, Federalist, Newsmax, OAN)
mechanisms.outcome: Daily Wire/FDRLST/Texas v. State filed Dec 2023 (censorship by proxy); GEC defunded/terminated ~Dec 2024
mechanisms.coordination_grade: Documented govt funding/promotion of private blacklists; alleged unconstitutional coercion (NRA v. Vullo line), not adjudicated

mechanisms.name: Operation Choke Point
mechanisms.outcome: Disclosed Aug 2013; House Oversight report 2014; FDIC reforms; ended Aug 2017
mechanisms.coordination_grade: Documented govt coercion via supervisory/reputational-risk leverage; deniable (no formal ban)

mechanisms.name: Pornhub/MindGeek processing cutoff
mechanisms.outcome: Dec 2020 card processing cut; ~10M+ unverified videos purged

mechanisms.name: OnlyFans ban + reversal
mechanisms.actor: Banks/processors (BNY Mellon, Metro Bank, JPMorgan) -> OnlyFans
mechanisms.outcome: Ban announced Aug 17 2021, reversed ~Aug 25 after assurances; Mastercard denied ordering

hypothesis_grade: PARTIALLY SUPPORTED - strong on mechanism, mixed on intent, weak on proven illegality
grade_reasoning: (a) Imposing controls government can't directly mandate is SUPPORTED for the financial/intermediary chain (Operation Choke Point; GEC->GDI/NewsGuard) and its private analog (Collective Shout exploiting the payment chokepoint), but NOT for advertiser coalitions (GARM, Stop Hate for Profit), which are private actors exercising their own speech/spending rights with no laundered government mandate. (b) Liability diffusion is SUPPORTED as a STRUCTURAL feature: across OnlyFans, Pornhub, Steam/itch every layer denies authorship (NGO 'only asked,' processors 'set general rules,' platforms 'had no choice'), making censorship un-attributable, unappealable, defendant-less. Coordination vs parallel conduct: openly organized boycotts = lawful; GARM's internal targeting (House report) looks coordinated but a federal court found it legally insufficient (X v. WFA dismissed WITH PREJUDICE Mar 26 2026). Most troubling category = government-adjacent coercion via private chokepoints (Choke Point, GEC/GDI). Overall: a recurring MECHANISM, not a single conspiracy.
